entries.name: Antiepileptics — mechanisms and principles
entries.memo: Stop the discharge: **block Na⁺/Ca²⁺** (excitation) or **enhance GABA** (inhibition).

segments.label: 1st generation
segments.value:
effective/often preferable, but ⚠️ **high teratogenicity** (not in pregnancy) and **strong CYP induction** (interactions). **2nd generation:** for lack of response/toxicity; usable in pregnancy.
• ⚠️ If a patient is controlled by an AED **do not switch drug** (≠ statins); on resumption the same one is reused.

freetext: **They do not cure** (↓ frequency/severity of seizures). **Mechanisms:** blockade of **Na⁺** channels (like local anesthetics); blockade of **T-type Ca²⁺** channels (absence seizures); enhancement of **GABA**; reduction of **glutamate** (few drugs).

entries.name: AED 1st gen — Na⁺ channel blockers (phenytoin, carbamazepine, oxcarbazepine)
entries.memo: **Phenytoin**: **zero-order** kinetics (saturable → abrupt toxicity); gingival hyperplasia, hirsutism.
freetext: **Phenytoin** (hydantoin): Na⁺ blocker (also **class Ib** antiarrhythmic); **very potent metabolic inducer**; ⚠️ "terrible" PK: protein binding ~100% (risk of **displacement**), **zero-order elimination** (saturable kinetics). Toxicity: cerebellar/ataxia, **gingival hyperplasia**, hirsutism, peripheral neuropathy, ⚠️ **teratogenic**. Outdated.
**Carbamazepine**: Na⁺ blockade (+ K⁺, + GABA); antiepileptic/mood stabilizer/**trigeminal neuralgia**; safer but **metabolic inducer**.
**Oxcarbazepine**: like carbamazepine but **without significant metabolic induction** (preferable).

entries.name: AED 1st gen — GABAergic and others (barbiturates, BDZ, ethosuximide, valproic acid)
entries.memo: **Ethosuximide** blocks the **thalamic T-type Ca²⁺ channels** = gold standard for **absence seizures**.

segments.label: Barbiturates (phenobarbital)
segments.value:
↑ **duration** of GABA-A Cl⁻ channel opening (TMD site → they open the channel even without GABA → overdose = **coma**); phenobarbital anticonvulsant (half-life >6h), preferable to BDZ **in children** (no cognitive slowing); **primidone** → metabolized to phenobarbital. ⚠️ tolerance, respiratory depression, ↓ renal flow.
• **Benzodiazepines** (clonazepam, clorazepate, diazepam, lorazepam): ↑ **frequency** of GABA-A; **diazepam** = status epilepticus; clonazepam/clorazepate = long term. *(Detail: file B1.)*
• **Ethosuximide** (succinimide): blockade of **T-type Ca²⁺** channels → **FIRST CHOICE in ABSENCE seizures**; manageable; ADR = hematologic alterations.
• **Valproic acid**: Na blockade + Ca-T + **↑ GABA** (↑ synthesis, ↓ GAT, ↓ GABA-transaminase) + ↓ glutamate (NMDA). Partial/generalized epilepsies + bipolar. ⚠️ **teratogenic** (spina bifida, cardiac/orofacial anomalies), fulminant hepatitis, ↑ weight, alopecia, tremor.

entries.name: AED 2nd generation
entries.memo: **Gabapentin/pregabalin** (α2δ): more neuropathic pain than epilepsy; **levetiracetam** simple, **lamotrigine → rash**.

segments.label: Gabapentin
segments.value: GABA analogue (competes with GAT) + Ca²⁺ P/Q blockade (↓ glutamate); epilepsy/mood stabilizer/**pain** (adjuvant); well tolerated.

segments.label: Lamotrigine
segments.value: modern version of phenytoin/carbamazepine; Na blockade (+ GABA/glutamate); usable in children (petit mal); ⚠️ allergic reactions → **Stevens-Johnson syndrome**.

segments.label: Topiramate
segments.value: Na + Ca blockade + **AMPA/kainate** antagonist + **carbonic anhydrase** inhibitor; partly replaces valproic acid; ADR: drowsiness, paresthesias, anorexia/↓ weight.

segments.label: Tiagabine
segments.value: GABA-mimetic (inhibits **GAT** — ≠ valproate which ↑ synthesis/↓ metabolism).

segments.label: Vigabatrin
segments.value: resistant epilepsy; ⚠️ hepatotoxic (not 1st line).

segments.label: Zonisamide
segments.value: Na + Ca + GABA; ⚠️ **the only one with neuroprotection/antioxidant**.

segments.label: Lacosamide
segments.value: Na blockade with ↑ **slow inactivation** (longer effect); excellent oral bioavailability.

segments.label: Felbamate
segments.value: Na + AMPA/kainate + GABA; ⚠️ **severe hepatotoxicity + aplastic anemia**.

segments.label: Levetiracetam
segments.value: binds the synaptic protein **SV2A** → normalizes GABA/glutamate exocytosis; partial/generalized + children; has displaced many AEDs.

segments.label: Brivaracetam
segments.value: higher SV2A affinity (overcomes polymorphisms), ↑ BBB penetration; refractory epilepsy. ⚠️ levetiracetam/brivaracetam → behavioral disturbances/psychosis in children <4 years.

segments.label: Perampanel
segments.value: non-competitive **AMPA** antagonist (↓ glutamate); half-life >100h; ⚠️ ↑ aggression (boxed warning). **Retigabine:** opens K⁺ channels (withdrawn — blue pigmentation). **Cannabidiol:** ↑ 2-AG (FAAH inhibition) + CB1 inverse agonist + 5-HT1A partial agonist (anxiolytic).

entries.name: Antiepileptics — indications by seizure type
entries.memo: **Absence seizures → ethosuximide**; in **status epilepticus** the first move is an **IV benzodiazepine**.

segments.label: 3 selection factors
segments.value: type of epilepsy, efficacy, safety.

segments.label: Spasms
segments.value: steroids, vigabatrin. **Absence seizures:** FIRST CHOICE **ethosuximide** (also valproate/lamotrigine, less effective). **Focal:** levetiracetam, brivaracetam. **Generalized:** **valproic acid** (the most used).

segments.label: Pregnancy
segments.value:
**SECOND** generation drugs.
## 🫀 STATINS AND LIPID-LOWERING AGENTS

entries.name: Statins — mechanism and pharmacokinetics
entries.memo: They block **HMG-CoA reductase** → the liver raises LDL receptors → **↓ LDL in the blood**.

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: inhibition of **HMG-CoA reductase** → ↓ mevalonate → ↑ LDL receptors (via **SREBP**) → ↓ LDL and total cholesterol. **Pleiotropic effects:** ↑ endothelial NOS (vasodilation/antiplatelet), anti-inflammatory/antioxidant, **plaque stabilization** → ↓ thromboembolic events.

segments.label: Molecular Target
segments.value: HMG-CoA reductase.

segments.label: Pharmacokinetic Parameters
segments.value: ⚠️ some inhibit **CYP3A4** (atorvastatin); simvastatin/lovastatin = **prodrugs**; ⚠️ high protein binding (95%, **pravastatin 50%** = safer); **administer before sleeping** (nocturnal synthesis); hepatic excretion (**pravastatin** renal → usable in hepatic impairment). Rosuvastatin = half-life ~19h, minimal CYP metabolism.

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: primary and secondary cardiovascular prevention (men >45, women >55, children >11 with familial hypercholesterolemia).

segments.label: Combinations
segments.value: + ezetimibe, + sequestrants, + fibrates (reduce the statin by 25%).

entries.name: Statins — classification, side effects and interactions
entries.nameShort: Statins — classification, side effects and interactions
entries.memo: Risk of **myopathy/rhabdomyolysis** (↑ CK), worsened by CYP3A4 inhibitors and by **grapefruit juice**.

segments.label: Classification
segments.value: **Level I** (↓ ~30-35%: simvastatin 10-40 mg, pravastatin, fluvastatin, lovastatin); **Level II** (↓ >50%: simvastatin 80 mg, atorvastatin, rosuvastatin).

segments.label: Adverse Reactions (ADR)
segments.value: ⚠️ **myalgia/RHABDOMYOLYSIS** (cerivastatin withdrawn), **hepatotoxicity** (↑ transaminases), GI discomfort, possible ↑ diabetes.

segments.label: Interactions
segments.value: ⚠️ **atorvastatin + erythromycin/clarithromycin** (both CYP3A4 inhibitors) → ↑ erythromycin (cardiac K⁺ blockade → **↑×6 risk of cardiac arrest**) and ↑ atorvastatin (→ **rhabdomyolysis**). Also **valproic acid** and **grapefruit juice** (CYP inhibitors) ↑ atorvastatin → rhabdomyolysis.

segments.label: Resistance / Efficacy Limits
segments.value: **rosuvastatin** = fewer interactions + ↑ HDL/↓ triglycerides (preferable but more expensive).

segments.label: Safety
segments.value: lovastatin/pravastatin/simvastatin (≤40 mg) well known; atorvastatin/simvastatin/lovastatin also in children with familial hypercholesterolemia.

entries.name: Other lipid-lowering agents (fibrates, nicotinic acid, cholestyramine, ezetimibe)
entries.memo: **Ezetimibe** (absorption), **fibrates** (king of triglycerides), **PCSK9 inhibitors** (LDL at historic lows).

segments.label: Nicotinic acid (niacin)/acipimox
segments.value: inhibit lipolysis, **↑ HDL**; ADR = cutaneous vasodilation (acipimox better tolerated).

segments.label: Cholestyramine
segments.value: bile acid sequestrant; ⚠️ ↓ absorption of **vitamins A/D/K** (coagulation → supplement); take other drugs 1-2h before or 4-6h after; ↑ triglyceridemia; poor compliance.

segments.label: Ezetimibe
segments.value: **selective** ↓ of dietary cholesterol absorption (does not interfere with vitamins); enterohepatic circulation (long duration); in combination with simvastatin.

freetext: **Fibrates** (clofibrate, gemfibrozil): **PPAR-α** agonists → ↑ fatty acid degradation/lipolysis → **↓ triglycerides** (choice in **hypertriglyceridemia**). ADR: GI, myalgia/myositis (⚠️ ↑ with simvastatin), rhabdomyolysis, gallstones.

entries.name: Hypertension — therapeutic strategy
entries.memo: Five families + thiazide diuretic; often a **fixed combination** to reach target with a single tablet.

segments.label: Regulatory systems (targets)
segments.value: **SNS** (locus coeruleus, rostral ventrolateral medulla), **RAAS** (juxtaglomerular β1 → renin), **kidney** (afferent arteriole). NSAIDs → afferent vasoconstriction → ↑ pressure.

segments.label: Classes
segments.value: sympatholytics (central), RAAS inhibitors, diuretics (kidney), agents active on heart/vessels.

segments.label: 1st choice
segments.value:
**RAAS inhibitors** (ACE-I, sartans), **calcium channel blockers**, **diuretics**, beta-blockers (in particular conditions). ⚠️ Central antihypertensives are **NOT 1st choice**.
• **2nd choice = combinations** (diuretic+beta-blocker, diuretic+ACE-I/sartan, Ca-blocker+beta-blocker/ACE-I/diuretic…). ⚠️ If the 1st-choice drug doesn't work → **do not switch/increase but COMBINE** (different sites of attack).

entries.name: Central sympatholytics — α2-agonists (clonidine, moxonidine, rilmenidine)
entries.memo: **Clonidine** = central α2 agonist (↓ sympathetic); **severe rebound** if abruptly discontinued.

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: **α2** agonists (presynaptic autoreceptor) → ↓ catecholamine release → ↓ vasomotor center/catecholaminergic discharge → ↓ resistance/output/pressure; ↓ renin (juxtaglomerular β1). **Clonidine/guanfacine/guanabenz** (+ imidazoline agonism); **moxonidine/rilmenidine** (2nd gen, **I1/I2** imidazoline agonists).

segments.label: Molecular Target
segments.value: α2-adrenergic; imidazoline receptors.

segments.label: Pharmacokinetic Parameters
segments.value: clonidine IV **slow** (rapid → ⚠️ paradoxical hypertensive peak from peripheral α2); moxonidine once-daily dosing.

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: hypertension (particular conditions); clonidine in **alcohol/opioid withdrawal**. **Moxonidine/rilmenidine** (I2) → ↑ renal flow/natriuresis, **↑ insulin response** (useful in the diabetic — ≠ clonidine), ↓ left ventricular hypertrophy.

segments.label: Common Adverse Reactions (ADR)
segments.value: clonidine → ⚠️ **rebound hypertension** on abrupt withdrawal (α2 up-regulation); asthenia, **depression**, vivid nightmares (⚠️ contraindicated in depressed/elderly patients). Moxonidine = excellent profile (no depression).

segments.label: Pharmacological Combinations / Adjuvants
segments.value: peripheral adrenergic blockers (**reserpine** — depletes vesicles, 1st antihypertensive in history; guanethidine). *Exam question: to demonstrate moxonidine's effect → give an **α2 antagonist** (e.g. yohimbine), not an imidazoline antagonist (the residual effect = imidazoline).*

entries.name: α-methyldopa
entries.memo: False neurotransmitter: **first choice in hypertension during pregnancy** (fetal safety proven over decades).

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: **false neurotransmitter** → converted into **α-methyl-norepinephrine** → stimulates presynaptic α2 (like clonidine) + ↓ sympathetic tone; ↓ dopamine.

segments.label: Molecular Target
segments.value: α2-adrenergic.

segments.label: Pharmacokinetic Parameters
segments.value: slow latency (~6h); duration ~10h.

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: hypertension, ⚠️ **used in PREGNANCY**.

segments.label: Common Adverse Reactions (ADR)
segments.value: drowsiness, depression, **parkinsonism**, orthostatic hypotension, **gynecomastia/galactorrhea/impotence**; ⚠️ **hepatotoxicity**, **hemolytic anemia (Coombs+)**; postpartum depression (↑ cytotoxic VEGF, oxidative stress, cerebral hypoperfusion).

segments.label: Resistance / Efficacy Limits
segments.value: slow latency.

segments.label: Pharmacological Combinations / Adjuvants
segments.value: N/A specific.

entries.name: α1-blockers (prazosin, doxazosin, tamsulosin)
entries.memo: ↓ pressure + prostatic unblocking (BPH); signature ADR = **first-dose orthostatic hypotension**.

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: reversible competitive antagonists of **α1** receptors (Gq → IP3/DAG → Ca²⁺/vasoconstriction) → arterial/venous **vasodilation**.

segments.label: Molecular Target
segments.value: **α1b** = arteriolar tone (antihypertensive → non-selective: **prazosin, doxazosin**, terazosin); **α1a** = prostate/bladder neck (BPH/LUTS → selective: **tamsulosin, silodosin, alfuzosin**).

segments.label: Pharmacokinetic Parameters
segments.value: take **in the evening** (⚠️ orthostatic hypotension/syncope); doxazosin slow, 24h.

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: hypertension (rarely 1st line, in combination); **BPH/LUTS**. Doxazosin → ↑ glucose tolerance (useful in the diabetic).

segments.label: Common Adverse Reactions (ADR)
segments.value: **orthostatic hypotension** (syncope), reflex tachycardia.

segments.label: Resistance / Efficacy Limits
segments.value: rarely 1st line.

segments.label: Pharmacological Combinations / Adjuvants
segments.value: in combination with other antihypertensives.

entries.name: Beta-blockers — generations and ancillary properties
entries.memo: **β1-cardioselective** (atenolol, bisoprolol, metoprolol) → safe in the bronchi; the non-selective ones are not (asthma).

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: β receptor antagonists. ⚠️ **β2** blockade impairs vasodilation/bronchodilation/insulin release/glycogenolysis.

segments.label: Generations
segments.value: **I** (non-selective: **propranolol**, nadolol, timolol — ⚠️ bronchoconstriction, ↓ insulin); **II** (**β1-selective/cardioselective**: atenolol, metoprolol — usable in asthmatics/diabetics); **III non-selective** (carvedilol, labetalol — **+ α1-block**); **III selective** (celiprolol, nebivolol).

segments.label: Ancillary properties
segments.value: **cardioselectivity**; **intrinsic sympathomimetic activity (ISA)** = β1 partial agonists (pindolol — also 5-HT1A partial agonist → anxiolytic); **lipophilicity** (→ CNS effects; choice for **tremors** — DD with Parkinson); **local anesthetic/membrane-stabilizing action** (propranolol, oxprenolol, acebutolol → Na/K channels; site = AV node). **Carvedilol** (β + α1-block, antioxidant/anti-remodeling); **celiprolol** (β1-antagonist + β2-agonist → vasodilation).

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: see next card.

segments.label: Adverse Reactions / Combinations
segments.value: see next card. ⚠️ handled by cytochromes → interactions.

entries.name: Beta-blockers — advanced mechanisms, indications and ADR
entries.nameShort: Beta-blockers — advanced mechanisms, indications and ADR
entries.memo: Life-saving **post-infarction**; in the diabetic they **mask hypoglycemia** and are never stopped abruptly.

segments.label: Biased agonists
segments.value: recruit **β-arrestins** → ERK/**eNOS** (nebivolol, carvedilol). **Inverse agonists:** inhibit the **spontaneous activity** (agonist-independent) of β1 → ⚠️ basis of use in congestive **HEART FAILURE** (also: ↓ endothelin, ↓ renin, ↓ sympathetic tone, ↓ apoptosis/oxidative stress/TNF-α).

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: hypertension (**3rd line** — NICE/USA; exception: those intolerant to ACE-I/sartans, women of childbearing age, ↑ sympathetic tone); **arrhythmias** (**class II** antiarrhythmics); **angina pectoris** (↓ rate → ↓ O₂ demand + ↑ diastole/perfusion); **secondary prevention of MI**; hypertrophic cardiomyopathy; **heart failure**; **migraine prophylaxis** (timolol/propranolol, triptan-like effect).

segments.label: Common Adverse Reactions (ADR)
segments.value: hypotensive crises, ⚠️ **AV block/asystole** (site = AV node), ↓ inotropism, ⚠️ **asthma** (non-selective), ↑ triglycerides/↓ HDL (β3), **↓ symptoms of hypoglycemia**, CNS effects (lipophilic: fatigue, ↓ libido, depression → not in young males); ⚠️ **withdrawal syndrome** (up-regulation → gradual discontinuation).

segments.label: Contraindications
segments.value: nodal dysfunction, bradycardia/AV block, heart failure (decompensated), **pheochromocytoma**, diabetes, young males, elderly African Americans.

segments.label: Pharmacological Combinations / Adjuvants
segments.value: ⚠️ interactions via CYP (maintain constant concentrations).

entries.name: RAAS — physiology and pharmacological targets
entries.memo: Renin → ACE → angiotensin II cascade; four blocking points: renin, ACE, AT1 receptor, aldosterone.

segments.label: Cascade
segments.value: **renin** (kidney; stimulated by **β1** and by ↓ Na of the **macula densa**) → angiotensinogen (hepatic) → **Angiotensin I** → **ACE** → **Angiotensin II**. Alternative pathways (**chymase**).

segments.label: Ang II receptors
segments.value: **AT1** (heart, vessels, kidney, adrenal → vasoconstriction, **aldosterone**); **AT2** (beneficial effects/vasodilation).

segments.label: ACE2
segments.value: converts Ang II → **Angiotensin 1-7** (cardioprotective, opposite effects to Ang II); it is the entry receptor of **SARS-CoV-2** (↓ Ang 1-7 → cardiovascular damage in COVID).

segments.label: Bradykinins
segments.value: substrate of ACE → its inhibition ↑ them (vasodilation via eNOS/NO if endothelium intact; cough/angioedema).

entries.name: ACE inhibitors (captopril, enalapril, ramipril…)
entries.memo: ↓ angiotensin II + ↑ bradykinin → signature ADR = **dry cough** and rare angioedema; **teratogenic**.

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: inhibition of **ACE** → ↓ Ang II → ↓ aldosterone; **↑ bradykinins** → vasodilation. ⚠️ In the long run ↓ response (chymase + loss of negative feedback → ↑ renin/Ang I).

segments.label: Molecular Target
segments.value: ACE (zinc-containing site).

segments.label: Classes
segments.value: sulfhydryl (**captopril** — first, short half-life, rapid peak; zofenopril); **carboxyl** (**enalapril** [prodrug→enalaprilat], lisinopril, quinapril, **ramipril** — half-life ~24h); phosphorus (fosinopril).

segments.label: Pharmacokinetic Parameters
segments.value: captopril/fosinopril → ↓ bioavailability with food; quinapril/ramipril → slow absorption, 24h (preferable). **Renal** elimination.

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: hypertension (**1st line**), heart failure, post-MI, **↓ proteinuria in the diabetic** (nephroprotection).

segments.label: Common Adverse Reactions (ADR)
segments.value: ⚠️ **dry cough + angioedema** (bradykinins); **hyperkalemia**; captopril → **lupus-like syndrome**, loss of taste, glycosuria.

segments.label: Resistance / Efficacy Limits
segments.value: ⚠️ **PREGNANCY** (teratogenic: oligohydramnios, cranial hypoplasia, fetal death); ⚠️ **CKD** (hyperkalemia + renal elimination → prefer sartans); bilateral renal artery stenosis, severe aortic stenosis, obstructive cardiomyopathy.

segments.label: Pharmacological Combinations / Adjuvants
segments.value: + thiazides (offset hyperkalemia and the restart of RAAS); + beta-blockers (post-MI); ⚠️ **Perindopril + Amlodipine** ("overused" combination). In pregnancy: α-methyldopa, propranolol.

entries.name: Sartans (losartan, valsartan, irbesartan, telmisartan, candesartan)
entries.memo: **AT1** antagonists: same efficacy as ACE inhibitors but **without the cough**.

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: **competitive antagonists of the AT1 receptor** → they block the Ang II signal on AT1; Ang II remains free → **negative feedback on renin**, binding to **AT2** (vasodilation), conversion to **Ang 1-7** (beneficial). **They do NOT involve bradykinins** → no cough/angioedema.

segments.label: Molecular Target
segments.value: AT1 receptor.

segments.label: Pharmacokinetic Parameters
segments.value: **hepatic** metabolism/elimination → usable in CKD.

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: hypertension, heart failure, post-MI; mild **uricosuric** effect. They replace ACE-I when ineffective or due to their side effects.

segments.label: Common Adverse Reactions (ADR)
segments.value: GI pain, headache; ⚠️ **teratogenic** (fetal toxicity). **NO cough/angioedema**.

segments.label: Resistance / Efficacy Limits
segments.value: more expensive than ACE-I.

segments.label: Pharmacological Combinations / Adjuvants
segments.value: other RAAS inhibitors: beta-blockers (β1 → ↓ renin), aldosterone antagonists (spironolactone/canrenone → file B11b).

entries.name: Renin inhibitors (aliskiren)
entries.memo: **Aliskiren** blocks the initial step, but **should not be combined** with ACE-i/sartans (worsens outcomes).

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: **false substrates** modeled on angiotensinogen → they bind competitively to the active site of renin without being cleaved.

segments.label: Molecular Target
segments.value: renin.

segments.label: Pharmacokinetic Parameters
segments.value: enalkiren/zankiren = low half-life/bioavailability (not used); **aliskiren** (FDA 2007) = higher half-life/bioavailability.

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: hypertension (monotherapy or combination).

segments.label: Common Adverse Reactions (ADR)
segments.value: N/A specific in the documents.

segments.label: Resistance / Efficacy Limits
segments.value: ⚠️ less effective than the other RAAS classes.

segments.label: Pharmacological Combinations / Adjuvants
segments.value: N/A specific.

entries.name: Calcium channel blockers (dihydropyridines, verapamil, diltiazem)
entries.memo: **-dipines to the vessels** (ankle edema), **verapamil to the heart** (never with a beta-blocker → complete AV block).

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: they block **voltage-dependent L-type Ca²⁺** channels (α1C subunit; different binding sites). **Dihydropyridines** (nifedipine, **amlodipine**, felodipine, nisoldipine): selective for the **VESSELS** (vessel:heart tissue-selectivity — amlodipine 10:1, nisoldipine 1000:1); **voltage-dependent** blockade; vasodilation; **not negatively chronotropic**. **Verapamil** (phenylalkylamine): predominant **CARDIAC** activity; **frequency-dependent** blockade → nodal → **negatively chronotropic**; ↓ intracellular Ca mobilization. **Diltiazem** (benzothiazepine): heart + vessels, negatively chronotropic.

segments.label: Molecular Target
segments.value: L-type Ca²⁺ channel (α1C).

segments.label: Pharmacokinetic Parameters
segments.value: amlodipine = slow absorption → no reflex tachycardia.

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: **hypertension** (dihydropyridines only, except electrical alterations), **angina pectoris** (all 3 classes → coronary vasodilation), **arrhythmias** (verapamil/diltiazem = **class IV** antiarrhythmics).

segments.label: Common Adverse Reactions (ADR)
segments.value: dihydropyridines → **edema, headache, reflex tachycardia** (nifedipine; amlodipine no); verapamil/diltiazem → ⚠️ **bradycardia/AV block**, ↓ inotropism.

segments.label: Pharmacological Combinations / Adjuvants
segments.value: ⚠️ **dihydropyridines + ACE-I** (the ACE-I counteracts reflex tachycardia and the restart of RAAS — e.g. Perindopril+Amlodipine).

entries.name: Heart failure — pathophysiological bases
entries.memo: The **neurohormonal damage is blocked**: ACE-i + beta-blocker + anti-aldosterone + SGLT2-i (the 'fab 4').

segments.label: β-arrestins
segments.value: they shift the G protein, excluding it from transduction → adrenaline binds β1 but does not ↑ cAMP → ↓ contraction → rationale for **beta-blockers** in heart failure.

segments.label: Calcium
segments.value: incorrect release/timing from the stores → excitation-contraction coupling fails. **Na⁺/Ca²⁺ antiporter** (extrudes Ca²⁺; Na⁺ enters along the gradient) whose drive is provided by the **Na⁺/K⁺-ATPase** pump.

segments.label: Strategy
segments.value: ↓ cardiac activity (beta-blockers), ↓ weight/pressure/Na⁺/water; then ↑ contraction with **digitalis glycosides**.

freetext: **Deficit of myocardial contractility** (remodeling, infarcted areas, hypertrophic/dilated cardiomyopathies); molecular mechanism not entirely known.

entries.name: Digitalis / digoxin
entries.memo: Blocks the Na⁺/K⁺-ATPase → **↑ contractility**; very narrow window → **xanthopsia (sees yellow)**, arrhythmias. Antidote: Digibind.

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: binds the **Na⁺/K⁺-ATPase** pump (E2-P state) competing with **K⁺** → ↑ intracellular Na⁺ → the Na/Ca exchanger **reverses** → **↑ Ca²⁺** → **positive inotrope**. ⚠️ hypokalemia ↑ effect (more free sites), hyperkalemia ↓ effect.

segments.label: Negative chronotrope
segments.value: ↑ vagal discharge (**M2** at the AV node → βγ dimer → opening of K⁺ channels, ↓ Ca²⁺ entry).

segments.label: Molecular Target
segments.value: Na⁺/K⁺-ATPase (α2/α3 muscle-cardiac-CNS isoforms; α1 renal not to be bound).

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: chronic heart failure; **antiarrhythmic** (↑ atrial rate → converts flutter→AF + block at the AV node).

segments.label: Common Adverse Reactions (ADR)
segments.value: ⚠️ **CNS toxicity = first sign** (confusion/disorientation → **drug holiday**); reentry arrhythmias (shorter potentials), AV block/asystole. Never ventricular tachycardia.

segments.label: Pharmacokinetic Parameters
segments.value: **digoxin** (preferred: poorly metabolized, reduced first-pass) vs **digitoxin** (⚠️ half-life >100h, accumulation, high protein binding → displacement) vs **ouabain** (parenteral, emergencies).

segments.label: Combinations / inotropic alternatives
segments.value: ⚠️ **dobutamine** (β1-agonist, acute only — too forceful) and PDE inhibitors (**amrinone, milrinone**); ACE-I, spironolactone, diuretics, nitrates.

entries.name: Theophylline and xanthines
entries.memo: Bronchodilator with mild cardiac effect; today marginal due to narrow window and CYP1A2 interactions.

segments.label: Mechanism of Action (positive inotrope)
segments.value: 1) **PDE inhibition** (sympathomimetic, ↑ effect of catecholamines); 2) **adenosine antagonism** (→ ↑ all cardiac properties, including rate); 3) **bronchodilation** (↑ cAMP); 4) effects on Ca²⁺ transport.

segments.label: Molecules
segments.value: caffeine, theobromine, **theophylline** (base of **aminophylline**, longer half-life); purine derivatives.

segments.label: Common Adverse Reactions (ADR)
segments.value: ⚠️ **cardiotoxic** (positive inotrope/chronotrope) → tea/coffee not advised in cardiac patients.

entries.name: Diuretics — overview (sites and classes)
entries.memo: Each class at a **nephron stop**: the more upstream you act, the more diuresis (but the more electrolytes lost).

segments.label: Na⁺ reabsorption
segments.value: proximal tubule **60%**, loop of Henle **25-30%**, distal tubule **7-10%**, collecting duct **2-6%** (aldosterone).

segments.label: Classes/sites
segments.value:
carbonic anhydrase inhibitors (proximal); **drastic/loop diuretics** (thick ascending limb); **thiazides** (distal); **K⁺-sparing** (collecting duct: Na channel inhibitors = amiloride/triamterene; aldosterone antagonists = spironolactone).
• ⚠️ **Key rule:** from **carbonic anhydrase inhibitors to thiazides → LOSS of K⁺**; **Na channel inhibitors + aldosterone antagonists → ↑ K⁺**.

entries.name: Carbonic anhydrase inhibitors (acetazolamide, methazolamide)
entries.memo: **Carbonic anhydrase** inhibitor: glaucoma and **mountain sickness** (the acidosis drives hyperventilation).

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: inhibition of carbonic anhydrase in the **proximal tubule** → ↓ Na⁺ reabsorption (coupled to bicarbonate) → ↑ excretion of **bicarbonate**, **alkalinization of the urine**, **hypokalemic metabolic acidosis**.

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: **glaucoma** (↓ aqueous humor), **epilepsy** (direct anticonvulsant + acidosis), prophylaxis of **mountain sickness**, edema, correction of diuretic-induced metabolic alkalosis, in combination (resistance to monotherapy).

segments.label: Common Adverse Reactions (ADR)
segments.value: ⚠️ **renal stones** (precipitation of Ca phosphate in alkaline urine), myelosuppression, cutaneous toxicity, allergic reactions, drowsiness, metabolic acidosis.

entries.name: Osmotic diuretics (mannitol)
entries.memo: **Osmotic diuretic**: pulls water out of tissues → **cerebral edema** and intracranial hypertension.

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: osmotic force in the lumen → ↓ reabsorption of the ultrafiltrate → ↑ elimination of Na⁺/water; acts on the **descending limb of the loop**; extracts water from the eye/brain.

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: glaucoma, **cerebral edema**, dialysis disequilibrium syndrome, children (also as laxative).

segments.label: Adverse Reactions / Contraindications
segments.value: ⚠️ **congestive heart failure** (they draw water → pulmonary edema), anuria, cranial hemorrhage, hepatic hypofunction. *(Slide topic — not covered in lecture.)*

entries.name: Loop / drastic diuretics (furosemide, bumetanide, ethacrynic acid, torasemide)
entries.memo: **Lasix = 'lasts six'**: life-saving in pulmonary edema, but it carries away **K⁺, Ca²⁺, Mg²⁺** (+ ototoxicity).

segments.label: Mechanism of Action
segments.value: inhibition of the **Na⁺/K⁺/2Cl⁻** symporter (thick ascending limb of the loop) → **global ion excretion** (Na⁺, K⁺, Cl⁻, Ca²⁺, Mg²⁺); ↑ renal flow (prostaglandins), ↑ renin/RAAS.

segments.label: Clinical Indications
segments.value: **acute pulmonary edema** ("white bloodletting"), chronic heart failure, hypertension, edema (nephrotic syndrome/cirrhosis/ascites), **hypercalcemia**, forced diuresis.

segments.label: Common Adverse Reactions (ADR)
segments.value: ⚠️ **hypokalemia/hypocalcemia/hypomagnesemia → arrhythmias**, hypochloremic alkalosis, **reversible ototoxicity/tinnitus** (ethacrynic acid > others), hyperuricemia, hyperglycemia, ↑ LDL-triglycerides/↓ HDL (metabolic syndrome), hyponatremia/hypovolemia.

segments.label: Resistance / Efficacy Limits
segments.value: ⚠️ only **attack therapy** (max 3-4 weeks → tolerance); not for chronic hypertension.

segments.label: Combinations / Interactions (⚠️ exam question)
segments.value: aminoglycosides/cisplatin (ototoxicity), anticoagulants (furosemide **fibrinolytic**), digitalis glycosides (arrhythmias from ↓ K/Ca), lithium/propranolol (↑ levels), sulfonylureas (hyperglycemia), NSAIDs (prostacyclin antagonism → contraindicated), amphotericin B (nephrotoxicity), probenecid (↓ excretion). Spironolactone = cardioprotective addition.

entries.name: Thiazide and K⁺-sparing diuretics
entries.memo: **They retain calcium** (useful in osteoporosis); the 'hypers' (glycemia, uricemia, lipids). Spironolactone → **gynecomastia**.

segments.label: Ca²⁺ peculiarity
segments.value: in the long term **↓ Ca²⁺ excretion** (+ action on **PTH**) → ⚠️ **perfect for hypertensive postmenopausal women** (protection from osteoporosis — **≠ furosemide** which loses Ca²⁺); hypomagnesemia.

segments.label: Antihypertensive mechanism
segments.value: ↓ volemia/preload → then compensation (SNS/RAAS) but maintenance of cardiac output; **direct vasodilatory effect** (HCTZ opens KCa channels; indapamide Ca-blocker-like). Antihypertensive activity **> drastic diuretics**; **profibrinic** effect (≠ furosemide fibrinolytic).

segments.label: K⁺-sparing agents
segments.value:
**amiloride/triamterene** (Na⁺ channel inhibitors); **spironolactone/canrenone** (competitive antagonists of **aldosterone**, cardioprotective).
### 📎 Appendix — Calcium channel blockers (completion of molecules)
• **Nicardipine** (selective for the coronaries, antianginal); **nimodipine** (lipophilic → **cerebral vasospasm after hemorrhage**); **mibefradil** (inhibitor of **L + T** channels; ⚠️ inhibits **P-gp/CYP3A4** → interactions).

segments.label: Active metabolites
segments.value:
norverapamil (renal elimination), deacyldiltiazem (hepatic). **Sublingual nifedipine** (Nifedicor) in emergencies.
• ⚠️ **Verapamil/diltiazem + beta-blockers = NO** (additive negative chronotrope); **dihydropyridines + beta-blockers/diuretics = OK**.

freetext: **Thiazides** (hydrochlorothiazide/HCTZ, chlorthalidone, indapamide): inhibition of the **Na⁺/Cl⁻** symporter (distal tubule); ⚠️ **↓ K⁺** (more distal Na⁺ → more exchange with K⁺).
